question_text: A student compares two electric heaters. Heater A converts 2000 J of input energy with an efficiency of 85%. Heater B converts 2000 J of input energy with an efficiency of 92%. How much more useful heat energy does Heater B produce compared to Heater A?
explanation: First calculate useful energy for each heater: Heater A = 2000 × 0.85 = 1700 J; Heater B = 2000 × 0.92 = 1840 J. The difference is 1840 - 1700 = 140 J. Although both heaters receive the same input energy, Heater B is more efficient and wastes less energy, producing 140 J more useful heat.
